column1: # A left-press in READY on a grid cell remembers the cell as the
# drag origin. The first drag motion onto a different cell promotes
# READY into POINT, anchored at the origin and free-end at the
# dragged-over cell. Subsequent drag motions only move the free end
# (anchor stays). Mouse-up does NOT exit POINT — it just ends the
# drag, the highlight persists so a follow-up command (icon Bold,
# /Range Format, etc.) can act on the range. Esc cancels the
# selection in the usual two-step way.
# Start at A1, READY, no live POINT state.
# Down on A1 — pointer moves there (it's already there); still READY.
# Drag to C3 — promote to POINT anchored at A1, free end at C3.
# Drag farther to D4 — anchor stays A1, free end follows.
# Drag back to B2 — range shrinks (anchor unchanged).
# Mouse-up — selection is finalized, mode and highlight persist.
# A subsequent click in POINT extends the existing anchor (Phase 2
# behavior — drag does not change that). Click C3.
# Esc unanchors; second Esc exits to READY.
# A press+drag while in POINT (entered via /RE) just live-extends —
# the press's cell is NOT used as a new anchor. Anchor stays at the
# Press B2 (Phase 2 — pointer moves, anchor C3 unchanged), drag D4.
# A drag that *starts* outside the grid (e.g. column-header row)
# must not promote to POINT. Move pointer somewhere known first,
# then attempt a drag rooted on the header row at y=4.
